summary:
Design a data structure that stores words and supports searching for them, including wildcard searches. This builds on a trie by allowing a query to match any single character.

Implement a WordDictionary class with two methods: addWord(word) stores a word, and search(word) returns true if any stored word matches the query. In a query, a '.' character is a wildcard that may match any single letter, so search must explore all branches that could satisfy the pattern.

examples.output: [null, null, null, null, false, true, true, true]
examples.explanation: search("pad") is false (never added). search("bad") is true. search(".ad") matches "bad", "dad", and "mad". search("b..") matches "bad" since the two dots cover any two letters following 'b'.
Only "a" is stored. Queries ".a", "a.", and "aa" all require two characters, so none match the single-letter stored word.

constraints: word in addWord consists of lowercase English letters
word in search consists of lowercase English letters or '.'
At most 2 dots appear in a single search query when word.length > 10
At most 10000 calls in total will be made to addWord and search
